title: rosa × hibernica var. setonensis
intro: Rosa × hibernica var. setonensis, commonly known as the Irish rose or Rosa setigera, is a variety of the Rosa × hibernica species, a hybrid of the Rosa centifolia and Rosa rubiginosa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, with its leaves and flowers exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Rosa × hibernica var. setonensis have been reported to provide relief from digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory problems, among other health benefits. Rosa × hibernica var. setonensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
